what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview card showing five status color treatments: success, warning, error, info, and pending, each with a tinted chip, dot, pill label, name, and hex value.
observations: Includes a @dsCard metadata comment for group, name, subtitle, and viewport.
Uses local CSS to define the card padding, lead text, five-column grid, swatch layout, chips, dots, pills, names, and hex labels.
Displays status foreground hex values for success #5A8A4A, warning #E5A547, error #C24A3C, info #4A78B8, and pending #E5C547.
Uses inline styles for each chip background, dot color, and pill text color.
Lead text states the palette is warm-shifted, desaturated, and intended to sit inside an ivory palette.
standouts: Pending is explicitly described as gold, not amber.
Each status combines a pale background, colored dot, label text color, semantic name, and hex readout.
The layout is a compact five-column swatch ledger within a preview card.
conventions: Imports shared _card.css and relies on shared CSS variables for fonts, radii, and foreground colors.
Keeps preview-specific CSS in a head-level style block.
Uses inline color declarations for the actual displayed swatch values.
Uses concise semantic status names as visible labels under each swatch.
concerns: Color values are hard-coded inline, so the preview does not expose whether these map to reusable tokens.
The five-column grid has no responsive adjustment inside this file.
Only the foreground status hex values are printed; chip background and pill text colors are not labeled.
notes: This file is a compact HTML preview documenting warm, desaturated status color treatments for a design-system card.
questions: Are the chip background colors intended to be formal tokens or only preview tints?
Are the pill text colors part of the status palette or local demonstration styling?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents warm-shifted success, warning, error, info, and pending colors, with pending shown as gold rather than amber.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make status colors legible as reusable visual decisions by showing each semantic state with its applied UI treatment and foreground hex value.
novelty: The file distinguishes pending as gold rather than amber and frames all status colors as adjusted to fit an ivory palette.
contribution: Provides a status-color preview substrate with semantic state labels, example chips, foreground hex values, and warm palette tuning that a fused system would keep as documented color treatment evidence.
summary_for_embedding: HTML design-system preview card rendering five warm-shifted status color swatches with labels, dots, pills, and foreground hex values.
intention_for_embedding: Created to document how success, warning, error, info, and pending status colors should appear inside the ConceptV ivory-oriented palette.
